output.explanation: Contains 'a.m.' but also an extra 'Dec 02' date fragment; most other a.m. values are only 'H:MM a.m.' without a date (e.g., '9:40 a.m.' / '11:50 a.m.').
Missing space before 'a.m.' relative to the dominant pattern 'H:MM a.m.' (e.g., '5:50 a.m.' exists) and has extra date fragment 'Dec 02'.
Includes 'Dec 02' after the time; most other a.m. values are just 'H:MM a.m.' without a date (e.g., '7:30 a.m.', '5:50 a.m.').
Has 'a.m.' plus 'Dec 02' after a time-of-day pattern not shared by the majority of values.
Has 'a.m. Dec 02' where the common structure is 'H:MM a.m.' (no date fragment).
Date fragment placed after 'a.m.' deviates from the dominant 'H:MM a.m.' pattern; resembles other 'Dec 02' appended variants.
Same issue: 'Dec 02' appended after 'a.m.'; majority are time-only like '12:15 a.m.' and '12:21 a.m.'
Missing space before 'a.m.' ('a.m.Dec 02' vs 'a.m.') and includes extra 'Dec 02' fragment; dominant form is time-only.
Uses numeric-date format '12/2/2011 ... a.m.'; most other a.m. values follow only 'H:MM a.m.' (no full date).
Uses numeric-date format '12/02/2011 ... a.m.' instead of time-only pattern seen in most values.
Uses numeric-date format '12/02/2011 ... a.m.'; inconsistent with predominant 'H:MM a.m.' values.
Uses numeric-date format with time '12:15 a.m.'; dominant pattern is time-only without '2011'.
Has 'a.m. Dec 02' with date fragment; common structure for a.m. entries is just 'H:MM a.m.'
Date fragment appended after a.m.; deviates from the dominant time-only format.
Missing space after time and includes 'Dec 02' fragment; does not match common 'H:MM a.m.' structure (e.g., '5:11 a.m.' and '5:11 a.m.Dec 02').
Full date mixed into value ('12/2/2011 5:50 a.m.') rather than time-only 'H:MM a.m.' seen for others.
Full date mixed into value ('12/02/2011 5:50 a.m.') rather than time-only 'H:MM a.m.'.
Year abbreviated/date prefix included ('12/2/11 12:21 a.m.') instead of time-only format used by other entries.
Year + date included ('12/2/2011 12:29 a.m.') instead of dominant time-only structure.
Includes date prefix in short form ('11/30 7:35 p.m.') rather than 'H:MM p.m.' pattern used by most p.m. values.
Includes date prefix ('11/30 6:40 p.m.') instead of time-only p.m. values.
Includes date prefix ('11/30 9:45 p.m.') instead of time-only p.m. pattern.
Missing space after time when date suffix is present ('5:11 a.m.Dec 02'); deviates from standard 'H:MM a.m.' (compare '5:11 a.m.' and 'Dec 02 5:50 a.m.' variants).
Uses full date with 'a.m.' prefix in short form ('12/2/11 5:11 a.m.') inconsistent with dominant time-only pattern.

Pattern mismatch: most entries use either 'H:MM a.m./p.m.' or 'H:MM a.m./p.m. Dec 02' with a consistent separator; here the value includes 'Dec 02' without matching the dominant 'Dec 02' positioning seen in similar multi-part entries (e.g., '5:50 a.m.  Fri 02-Dec-2011' and '12:21 a.m. Dec 02').

Structure deviation: mixes day-of-week + month/day + time in a format not shared by the dominant majority (others are either 'H:MM a.m./p.m.' or 'H:MM a.m./p.m. Dec 02' or 'H:MM a.m.  Fri 02-Dec-2011'); this includes 'Fri Dec 2 12:29 a.m.'.

Minor format inconsistency relative to other single-part time strings: includes 'a.m.' but not the consistent spacing seen in 'H:MM a.m.' values; still syntactically parseable, but deviates from the common 'H:MM a.m.' pattern by being less standard than most.

Date/time separator pattern mismatch: other '11/30 ...' values exist, but most time+meridiem entries use 'H:MM p.m.' or 'H:MM a.m.'; here uses '11/30 7:53 p.m.' which deviates from the dominant format set for this column.
Format deviation: '11/30 6:40 p.m.' does not match the predominant single time/Dec-02 patterns used elsewhere (e.g., '6:40 p.m.' and 'H:MM ... Dec 02'); it embeds a date with slashes.

Invalid characters/structure: missing spaces between time and meridiem/date block and includes a '12-01-11' suffix without the consistent 'a.m./p.m. Dec 02' or 'Fri 02-Dec-2011' pattern. Compared with '10:34A 12-01-11' etc., this block uses an inconsistent concatenation style.
Invalid characters/structure: concatenated '10:34A 12-01-11' lacks standard 'a.m./p.m.' token with spacing; deviates from the dominant time formats.
Invalid characters/structure: '7:55P 12-01-11' uses 'P' directly attached to time; most values use 'p.m.' or 'a.m.' with spacing.
Invalid characters/structure: '9:20A 12-01-11' uses 'A' directly attached; dominant format uses 'a.m.' token.
Invalid characters/structure: '11:10A 12-01-11' uses attached 'A' abbreviation; deviates from common 'a.m.' pattern.
Pattern non-conformity: 'Thu  22:15' uses 24-hour time ('22:15') without a.m./p.m., unlike almost all other entries which use 12-hour with 'a.m.'/'p.m.' or include 'Dec 02' with meridiem.
Pattern non-conformity: 'Thu  12:18' lacks a.m./p.m. marker while other time-only values include 'a.m.'/'p.m.'.
Pattern non-conformity: 24-hour-style '17:55' without a.m./p.m.; differs from prevalent 12-hour + meridiem formats.
Pattern non-conformity: 'Thu  19:25' uses 24-hour time without a.m./p.m.; most values are 12-hour with meridiem.
Pattern non-conformity: 'Thu  7:45' lacks a.m./p.m.; dominant entries include meridiem.
Pattern non-conformity: missing a.m./p.m. in 'Thu  6:55' while most times include meridiem.
Pattern non-conformity: 'Thu  10:35' lacks a.m./p.m.; dominant pattern uses meridiem.
Minor format inconsistency: compared with other 'Fri 02-Dec-2011' variants, this one omits the 'Fri 02-Dec-2011' suffix; structural mismatch with the related group may indicate truncation.
Invalid characters/structure: '9:40aDec 1' concatenates time, meridiem ('a') and date without spaces; most other date+time strings include 'a.m.'/'p.m.' with separators.
Invalid characters/structure: missing spacing between 'a' and 'Dec 1' ('10:40aDec 1').
Invalid characters/structure: '8:00aDec 1' lacks spaces and uses single-letter 'a' rather than 'a.m.'.
Invalid characters/structure: '6:40aDec 1' same concatenation issue and uses 'a' not 'a.m.'.
Invalid characters/structure: '5:35aDec 1' concatenated and uses single-letter 'a'.

Pattern non-conformity: '11/30 9:10 p.m.' uses 'M/D' with slashes; other entries use either standalone 'H:MM p.m.' or 'H:MM ... Dec 02'.
